posting.sourceTitle: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund (formerly known as New England Carpenters Apprenticeship Fund and Northeast Carpenters Apprenticeship Fund)
posting.sponsorName: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund
posting.locationSummary: Rochester, NY area and a multi-county regional jurisdiction in upstate New York
posting.applicationSummary: Apply online during the recruitment period after attending a required in-person information session and receiving the passcode needed to complete the application.
posting.applicationHours: For application help: Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 17 years old.
posting.residencyRequirement: Must live in the geographical jurisdiction covering Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, Niagara, Orleans, Genesee, Wyoming, Monroe, Livingston, Wayne, Ontario, Chemung, Schuyler, Steuben, Tompkins, Yates, Seneca, Cayuga, Cortland, Onondaga, Madison, Oswego, Broome, Delaware, Chenango, Otsego, Tioga, Oneida, Herkimer, Jefferson, Lewis, and St. Lawrence counties.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation and be willing to travel to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug test after selection and before enrollment; the test is at the sponsor's expense. Applicants who test positive cannot reapply for one year.
posting.classRequirement: Must be willing to attend required classes at the approved school.
allRequirements: Must be at least 17 years old.

Must attest in writing that they are physically able to perform the work of a Carpenter, including working on scaffolds and in confined spaces, working in inclement weather, performing constant repetitive motions, climbing and working from heights, lifting at least 50 lbs., and standing and stooping for prolonged periods.
Must pass a drug test, at the sponsor's expense, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship. If an applicant tests positive, they cannot reapply for one year.
Must be willing to attend required classes at the approved school.
Must have reliable means of transportation and be willing to travel to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.

Must live in the geographical jurisdiction that includes Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, Niagara, Orleans, Genesee, Wyoming, Monroe, Livingston, Wayne, Ontario, Chemung, Schuyler, Steuben, Tompkins, Yates, Seneca, Cayuga, Cortland, Onondaga, Madison, Oswego, Broome, Delaware, Chenango, Otsego, Tioga, Oneida, Herkimer, Jefferson, Lewis, and St. Lawrence counties.
Must provide a military transfer card or discharge form DD-214, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 17, live in the covered county area, have reliable transportation, attend required classes, pass a drug test after selection, and be physically able to do carpenter work.
posting.contactName: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund
posting.contactSummary: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund, (585) 436-1110, for application help and further information.
